tab.title: Coding Rules
tab.desc: Define the coding rules for this project. Your AI coding tool follows them while it writes code.

tab.skill_delete_message: Permanently delete the '{name}' skill from your library?
tab.import_no_skills: There are no skills to import from the current project.
tab.import_failed: Failed to save to the library.
tab.export_done: Imported {count} skills into the current project.

outlet.title: Where these coding rules are used

library.empty_desc: Collect your best skills here and bring them into new projects with a single click.

library.empty_folder: No skills in this folder yet.
library.detail_empty_html:

see its details.

edit_dialog.instruction_placeholder: Type a new instruction and press Enter
edit_dialog.tag_placeholder: Type a tag and press Enter (e.g. react, eslint)

quality.title_ok: A little more polish would help
quality.good_msg: The AI can apply this skill consistently. The instructions are clear and well structured 👍

import_conflict.title: Same-ID skill conflict — {count} found
import_conflict.message_html: The current project '{name}' already has skills with the same ID. Please choose how to handle them.
import_conflict.skip_desc: Exclude conflicting skills and import the rest. Keeps the current project intact.
import_conflict.overwrite_desc: Replace the current project's skills with the library values. Warning: cannot be undone.
import_conflict.rename_desc_html: Assign conflicting skills a new ID with a '-copy' suffix. Keeps both.

folder.name_hint: 💡 Letters, numbers, spaces, '-' and '_' allowed (up to 50 characters)
folder.desc_placeholder: e.g. React + TypeScript standard skill collection
folder.category_hint: 💡 Used for search / filtering. Free text (letters/numbers/spaces/'-'/'_').
folder.delete_empty: This folder is empty. Delete it now?
folder.delete_has_skills_html: This folder contains {count} skills. How would you like to handle them?
folder.delete_cascade_desc: Permanently delete the folder and all {count} skills inside
folder.delete_move_desc: Delete only the folder. Skills are preserved.

editor.trigger_placeholder: e.g. When writing a Spring Boot Controller
editor.new_instruction_placeholder: Type a new instruction and press Enter...
editor.tags_desc_html: Tags automatically link this rule to the matching services.
editor.new_tag_placeholder: Type a tag and press Enter
editor.applied_services_desc: Services currently matched to this skill's tags and applied. (read-only)

ai_reco.title: AI Skill Suggestions
ai_reco.intro_html: The AI reads this project's CPS (policy spec) and PRD (requirements doc), then automatically picks the skills you need from the local .skills catalog. Already registered skills are automatically excluded from suggestions.
ai_reco.filter_category_hint: Leave empty to suggest from all categories.
ai_reco.filter_folder_hint: Leave empty to suggest from the entire library.
ai_reco.no_library_folders: There are no folders in your library. Add skills from "My Library" first.

mcp_guide.headline: Connect Your AI Coding Agent
mcp_guide.sub: Cursor / Claude Code reads your PRD, Design, and Spec directly to help you code.
mcp_guide.step1_desc: A connection method that lets AI coding agents like Cursor / Claude Code access your project's PRD, Design, and Spec directly. Connect once and the agent references your spec on its own.
mcp_guide.step1_tip: After connecting, the agent calls it automatically in the background — no need to copy and paste your spec every time.
mcp_guide.step2_desc: Plain Cursor: "What does this function do?" → guesses from code alone. With MCP: the same question → answers precisely "This is the Aggregate Order implementation of Story-01.1 (order processing)."
mcp_guide.step2_tip: Cursor gets instant access to your spec data that ChatGPT / Copilot can never see — a real differentiator.
mcp_guide.step3_desc: 1) On the /profile page, copy the JSON from the "Connect AI coding agent" card  →  2) Paste it into Cursor's ~/.cursor/mcp.json (or Claude Code's project .mcp.json)  →  3) Restart Cursor.
mcp_guide.step3_tip: One click of the "Copy" button on the /profile card puts JSON filled with your own token onto the clipboard.
mcp_guide.step4_desc: Eleven read-only tools the agent calls automatically: lineage (file↔Story mapping, Design tracing, node list, Story details, Spec search) / planning docs (PRD, CPS, coding rules) / design contracts (API, screen, lint findings).
mcp_guide.step4_tip: You don't need to memorize tool names — Cursor picks and calls the right tool on its own.
mcp_guide.step5_desc: Open the "Connect AI coding agent (MCP)" card on the /profile page to get the endpoint URL and config JSON. Cursor users can finish connecting in a minute.
mcp_guide.step5_tip: Requires Cursor 0.45+ or Claude Code. Both are free / official channels.

skill_library_api.import_failed: Failed to import from the project.
skill_library_api.export_failed: Failed to export to the project.
